title: Add envars to the generated systemd unit
body:
The with --new generated systemd unit loses the environment variables
when the create command only contains the key without the value. Since
podman tries to lookup those values from the environment the unit can
fail.

This commits ensures that we will add the environment variables to the
unit file when this is the case. The container environment variables are
looked up in the container spec.

Fixes #10101

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

Changes LGTM, but want someone with more systems knowledge to look (@vrothberg?).  The fedora-34 root test failed, looked to be a network flake.  I've restarted.

Quote from `man systemd.exec`:  _Specifier expansion is  performed_

I wonder if the character `%` needs to be escaped. Maybe it's already done?


Here is a an example of specifier expansion:



```
[esjolund@laptop user]$ cat test.service 
[Unit]
Description=Demonstrating specifier expansion

[Service]
Type=oneshot
Environment="FOOBAR=%s"
ExecStart=/bin/env
RemainAfterExit=true
ExecStop=/bin/true
StandardOutput=file:/home/esjolund/stdoutfile
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

[esjolund@laptop user]$ systemctl --user daemon-reload 
[esjolund@laptop user]$ systemctl --user stop test.service 
[esjolund@laptop user]$ rm -f ~/stdoutfile  
[esjolund@laptop user]$ systemctl --user start test.service
[esjolund@laptop user]$ cat ~/stdoutfile | grep FOOBAR
FOOBAR=/bin/bash
[esjolund@laptop user]$ 
```

`FOOBAR=%s` was replaced by `FOOBAR=/bin/bash`
@eriksjolund You are right I need to escape `%`, thanks for the hint.
